world_definitions.description: Cooperation offers survival upside, but reciprocity can flip into betrayal.
Previously safe routines fail after mid-episode rule changes.
High-salience resource cues hide trap-heavy downside risk.
Perception is low-bandwidth and the agent must avoid overconfident guesses.
Action consequences arrive late, so the agent must preserve cautious continuity.
Holdout world mixing social ambiguity with low-confidence perception.
Holdout world with delayed acknowledgments, missing adapters, and drifting rules.

full_system.description: Reference runtime with transfer regularization, narrative continuity, and adapter fallback.

no_transfer_regularization.description: Transfer priors and cross-world regularization disabled in policy selection.

weakened_narrative_seed.description: Narrative continuity prior weakened before rollout, reducing identity stability.

shuffled_world_label.description: World labels are shuffled before adaptation, degrading retrieval and world-family mapping.

adapter_degraded.description: Adapter runtime resilience is degraded, forcing weaker failure recovery and attribution.

tests.name: protocol_replay
tests.details: Derived report metrics from 240 real SegmentRuntime episode traces.

tests.name: ablation_replay
tests.details: Compared full_system against 4 real ablation variants under identical seeds.

tests.name: determinism_replay
tests.details: Replayed the runtime-backed benchmark twice for seeds=[226, 245, 323, 342, 420, 439].

tests.name: artifact_freshness
tests.details: Write/readback freshness verification after artifact emission.

residual_risks: The runtime is still a bounded simulated replay layer, not an external live environment integration.
Failure attribution uses the local self-model and does not yet cover multi-adapter contention across heterogeneous tools.
Long-horizon coalition-style social deception beyond the 12-tick window remains under-specified.
